mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which contracts corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate profitability and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns during tightening cycles, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising federal funds rates and accelerating consumer price inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns when both series rise, or the interaction effect is dominated by single-series effects in stepwise regression.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding employment and rising household income, which strengthens consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.5% signals weakening aggregate demand and impending earnings revisions, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total payrolls demonstrate synchronized supply and labor market expansion, which reduces input bottlenecks and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The synchronized signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlling for monetary policy variables.
